step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the conclusion reached in Round 1. One additional point worth noting is that maintaining a consistent thickness around the apical area ensures uniform healing and reduces the risk of thermal injury due to heat generated during cementation procedures. This consistency also aids in better tissue integration post-surgery.

Final Answer Choice: B To maintain optimal conditions without risking complications, 3 mm remains the ideal depth for MTA in root-end fillings.
